skill.name: skill-creator
skill.description: Guides agents through planning, drafting, packaging, and validating Claude or Codex skills with concise structures and progressive disclosure.
skill.summary: Create and refine AI agent skills using structured guidance and validation checkpoints.
skill.author: Doyajin174

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby backticks, weak cryptography, and entropy are false positives caused by Markdown fences, Korean text, and documentation examples. The skill is not malicious, but it intentionally permits Bash, Edit, and Write workflows for creating skill files, so publication should include a tooling warning.

medium_findings.title: Legitimate Bash and File Editing Capability
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash, Edit, and Write tool access and instructs agents to run local packaging scripts. This supports the intended skill creation workflow, but it can modify files and execute commands in the user's workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines directly show Bash and file editing permissions plus local script execution examples. The context is legitimate developer tooling, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Markdown Fences Misclassified as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick findings are false positives. The cited lines are Markdown code fences, directory trees, YAML examples, and documentation snippets, not executable Ruby shell interpolation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file content shows fenced Markdown examples rather than Ruby code. No evidence found of Ruby source, command substitution, or user-controlled shell construction.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm matches occur inside prose, YAML examples, and a validation checklist. No cryptographic functions, hashes, ciphers, or security-sensitive algorithm choices are present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain description text and examples about skill metadata. I found no evidence of cryptographic implementation or algorithm selection.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Heuristic Caused by Multilingual Markdown
low_findings.description: The entropy warning is a false positive. The file is readable Markdown with Korean and English documentation, not binary, encrypted, or obfuscated content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found normal Markdown structure across the file. The higher entropy is plausibly explained by multilingual UTF-8 text and box drawing characters.

content.value_statement: Building reliable AI skills requires clear scope, concise instructions, and useful reusable resources. This skill guides agents through planning, writing, packaging, and validating skill folders.
actual_capabilities: Explains the structure of a valid skill folder.
Guides creation of SKILL.md frontmatter and body instructions.
Recommends when to use scripts, references, and assets.
Provides examples for progressive disclosure patterns.
Includes validation checklist items for basic quality review.
Shows packaging and initialization command patterns.
limitations: It does not include working helper scripts in the skill package.
It does not validate generated skills by itself.
Most guidance is written in Korean with some English headings.
It assumes users understand local command execution risks.

use_cases.title: Create a new internal skill
use_cases.description: Plan the folder structure, metadata, core instructions, and reusable resources for a new agent skill.

use_cases.title: Improve an existing skill
use_cases.description: Review a skill for concise instructions, better triggering, and clearer resource organization.

use_cases.title: Teach skill design standards
use_cases.description: Use the checklist and examples to explain skill anatomy and progressive disclosure to contributors.

prompt_templates.title: Start a simple skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new skill scaffold.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to help me plan a new AI skill for handling PDF cleanup tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Refine skill instructions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my SKILL.md and suggest concise changes that improve triggering and workflow clarity.

prompt_templates.title: Plan reusable resources
prompt_templates.scenario: Decide what files belong in scripts, references, and assets.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to decide which reusable resources my database reporting skill should include.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a complex skill design
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before publishing a skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to evaluate whether my multi-domain deployment skill uses progressive disclosure correctly.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for editing invoices.
output: A proposed skill name and description.
A short workflow for invoice editing tasks.
A resource plan for templates, validation notes, and sample documents.

output_examples.input: Review this skill for quality before publishing.
output: A concise assessment of trigger clarity.
Suggested edits for reducing unnecessary content.
Validation checklist results and remaining concerns.

best_practices: Define when the skill should trigger in the description field.
Keep SKILL.md concise and move detailed material into references.
Test any included scripts before publishing the skill.
anti_patterns: Do not add unrelated README files or process notes.
Do not place detailed reference material only in SKILL.md.
Do not publish command examples without explaining user workspace impact.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create and improve AI agent skills with clear metadata, workflow instructions, and optional reusable resources.

faq.question: Can it generate a complete skill automatically?
faq.answer: It provides guidance and command patterns, but the agent must still create files and validate the result.

faq.question: Does this skill include executable scripts?
faq.answer: No. It references script locations and commands, but this package only contains SKILL.md.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The content is not malicious, but it permits Bash and file editing workflows for skill creation.

faq.question: Is it suitable for Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance applies to Claude, Claude Code, and Codex style skill workflows.

faq.question: What should users review before running it?
faq.answer: Users should review generated file paths, script commands, and any workspace changes before execution.
